---
title: "Stock Indices Interactive Charts"
format:
  html:
    theme: [cosmo, theme.scss]
    page-layout: full
    self-contained: false
---

<head>
  <meta name="description" content="Interactive charting for multiple stock indices on a single scrollable page.">
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;600&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
</head>

<body>

## Apple Inc. (AAPL)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-aapl" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">AAPL</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Apple Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-aapl" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'aapl')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-aapl" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'aapl')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-aapl" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'aapl')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-aapl" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'aapl')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-aapl" onclick="toggleFullscreen('aapl')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-aapl
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_aapl = pd.read_csv("../downloads/aapl_enriched.csv")
df_aapl['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_aapl['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_aapl = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_aapl = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_aapl.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_aapl.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_aapl['date'], open=df_aapl['open'], high=df_aapl['high'], low=df_aapl['low'], close=df_aapl['close'],
        name='AAPL Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_aapl.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_aapl['date'], y=df_aapl['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_aapl, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_aapl = [{'date': '2011-08-24', 'label': 'Jobs Resigns / Cook CEO', 'desc': 'Steve Jobs resigns due to health reasons; Tim Cook is appointed CEO. Jobs passes away shortly after on October 5, 2011.'}, {'date': '2014-06-09', 'label': '7-for-1 Stock Split', 'desc': 'Apple implements a 7-for-1 stock split to make shares more accessible to retail investors.'}, {'date': '2020-08-31', 'label': '4-for-1 Stock Split', 'desc': 'Apple conducts another stock split (4-for-1) amid massive market expansion.'}, {'date': '2023-06-30', 'label': '$3T Market Cap', 'desc': 'Apple becomes the first publicly traded company to close with a market capitalization exceeding $3 trillion.'}]

for ev in events_aapl:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_aapl['date'][df_aapl['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_aapl.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_aapl[df_aapl['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_aapl['high'].mean()
        
        fig_aapl.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_aapl.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_aapl.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_aapl.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Apple Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Apple Inc. was founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. Initially focusing on personal computers, the company transformed into a consumer electronics giant with the launches of the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. Today, it is a global leader in technology, hardware design, and digital services.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2011-08-24 (Jobs Resigns / Cook CEO):</strong> Steve Jobs resigns due to health reasons; Tim Cook is appointed CEO. Jobs passes away shortly after on October 5, 2011.</li>
      <li><strong>2014-06-09 (7-for-1 Stock Split):</strong> Apple implements a 7-for-1 stock split to make shares more accessible to retail investors.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-08-31 (4-for-1 Stock Split):</strong> Apple conducts another stock split (4-for-1) amid massive market expansion.</li>
      <li><strong>2023-06-30 ($3T Market Cap):</strong> Apple becomes the first publicly traded company to close with a market capitalization exceeding $3 trillion.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Starbucks Corporation (SBUX)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-sbux" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">SBUX</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Starbucks Corporation</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-sbux" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'sbux')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-sbux" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'sbux')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-sbux" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'sbux')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-sbux" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'sbux')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-sbux" onclick="toggleFullscreen('sbux')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-sbux
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_sbux = pd.read_csv("../downloads/sbux_enriched.csv")
df_sbux['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_sbux['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_sbux = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_sbux = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_sbux.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_sbux.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_sbux['date'], open=df_sbux['open'], high=df_sbux['high'], low=df_sbux['low'], close=df_sbux['close'],
        name='SBUX Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_sbux.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_sbux['date'], y=df_sbux['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_sbux, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_sbux = [{'date': '2017-04-03', 'label': 'Johnson Appointed CEO', 'desc': 'Kevin Johnson takes over as CEO from long-time leader Howard Schultz, shifting focus to technology, mobile ordering, and cold beverage innovation.'}, {'date': '2020-03-12', 'label': 'COVID store closures', 'desc': 'Starbucks closes company-operated stores across North America to curb COVID-19, causing a sharp drop in short-term sales and share prices.'}, {'date': '2022-04-04', 'label': 'Schultz returns interim CEO', 'desc': 'Howard Schultz returns as interim CEO to lead a restructuring program and address growing unionization efforts.'}]

for ev in events_sbux:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_sbux['date'][df_sbux['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_sbux.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_sbux[df_sbux['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_sbux['high'].mean()
        
        fig_sbux.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_sbux.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_sbux.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_sbux.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Starbucks Corporation Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Starbucks Corporation was founded in Seattle, Washington in 1971. Under Howard Schultz's leadership in the 1980s, the company adopted the Italian espresso bar concept, leading to rapid global expansion. Starbucks is now the world's largest coffeehouse chain, known for popularizing dark-roasted coffee and creating a 'third place' between home and work.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2017-04-03 (Johnson Appointed CEO):</strong> Kevin Johnson takes over as CEO from long-time leader Howard Schultz, shifting focus to technology, mobile ordering, and cold beverage innovation.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-03-12 (COVID store closures):</strong> Starbucks closes company-operated stores across North America to curb COVID-19, causing a sharp drop in short-term sales and share prices.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-04-04 (Schultz returns interim CEO):</strong> Howard Schultz returns as interim CEO to lead a restructuring program and address growing unionization efforts.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

## Microsoft Corporation (MSFT)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-msft" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">MSFT</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Microsoft Corporation</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-msft" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'msft')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-msft" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'msft')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-msft" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'msft')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-msft" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'msft')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-msft" onclick="toggleFullscreen('msft')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-msft
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_msft = pd.read_csv("../downloads/msft_enriched.csv")
df_msft['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_msft['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_msft = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_msft = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_msft.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_msft.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_msft['date'], open=df_msft['open'], high=df_msft['high'], low=df_msft['low'], close=df_msft['close'],
        name='MSFT Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_msft.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_msft['date'], y=df_msft['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_msft, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_msft = [{'date': '2014-02-04', 'label': 'Nadella appointed CEO', 'desc': "Satya Nadella is named CEO, succeeding Steve Ballmer. Nadella initiates a 'cloud-first, mobile-first' strategy, reviving Microsoft's market leadership."}, {'date': '2016-06-13', 'label': 'LinkedIn Acquisition', 'desc': 'Microsoft announces the acquisition of LinkedIn for $26.2 billion, expanding its enterprise network services.'}, {'date': '2023-01-23', 'label': 'OpenAI Partnership Boost', 'desc': 'Microsoft announces a multi-year, multi-billion dollar investment in OpenAI, accelerating the integration of generative AI into its Windows, Office, and Azure products.'}]

for ev in events_msft:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_msft['date'][df_msft['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_msft.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_msft[df_msft['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_msft['high'].mean()
        
        fig_msft.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_msft.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_msft.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_msft.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Microsoft Corporation Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Microsoft Corporation was founded in 1975 by Paul Allen and Bill Gates. It dominated the personal computer operating system market with MS-DOS and Windows. Under Satya Nadella, the company successfully pivoted to cloud computing, enterprise services, and artificial intelligence, positioning it as one of the world's most valuable corporations.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2014-02-04 (Nadella appointed CEO):</strong> Satya Nadella is named CEO, succeeding Steve Ballmer. Nadella initiates a 'cloud-first, mobile-first' strategy, reviving Microsoft's market leadership.</li>
      <li><strong>2016-06-13 (LinkedIn Acquisition):</strong> Microsoft announces the acquisition of LinkedIn for $26.2 billion, expanding its enterprise network services.</li>
      <li><strong>2023-01-23 (OpenAI Partnership Boost):</strong> Microsoft announces a multi-year, multi-billion dollar investment in OpenAI, accelerating the integration of generative AI into its Windows, Office, and Azure products.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-csco" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">CSCO</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Cisco Systems, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-csco" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'csco')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-csco" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'csco')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-csco" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'csco')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-csco" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'csco')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-csco" onclick="toggleFullscreen('csco')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-csco
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_csco = pd.read_csv("../downloads/csco_enriched.csv")
df_csco['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_csco['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_csco = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_csco = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_csco.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_csco.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_csco['date'], open=df_csco['open'], high=df_csco['high'], low=df_csco['low'], close=df_csco['close'],
        name='CSCO Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_csco.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_csco['date'], y=df_csco['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_csco, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_csco = [{'date': '2015-07-26', 'label': 'Robbins becomes CEO', 'desc': "Chuck Robbins succeeds John Chambers as CEO, accelerating Cisco's shift toward software-defined networking, cloud security, and subscription models."}, {'date': '2020-03-11', 'label': 'WFH Hardware Spike', 'desc': 'The onset of COVID-19 drives high demand for enterprise networking hardware and Webex collaboration software to support remote work setups.'}, {'date': '2023-09-21', 'label': 'Splunk Acquisition Announced', 'desc': 'Cisco announces the acquisition of Splunk for $28 billion to bolster its data analytics, AI observability, and cybersecurity portfolios.'}]

for ev in events_csco:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_csco['date'][df_csco['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_csco.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_csco[df_csco['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_csco['high'].mean()
        
        fig_csco.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_csco.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_csco.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_csco.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Cisco Systems, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Cisco Systems, Inc. was founded in 1984 by Stanford University computer scientists Leonard Bosack and Sandy Lerner. Cisco pioneered the concept of local area networks (LAN) connecting geographically disparate computers over multiprotocol router systems. It remains a key infrastructure provider for global networking, cybersecurity, and cloud integrations.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2015-07-26 (Robbins becomes CEO):</strong> Chuck Robbins succeeds John Chambers as CEO, accelerating Cisco's shift toward software-defined networking, cloud security, and subscription models.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-03-11 (WFH Hardware Spike):</strong> The onset of COVID-19 drives high demand for enterprise networking hardware and Webex collaboration software to support remote work setups.</li>
      <li><strong>2023-09-21 (Splunk Acquisition Announced):</strong> Cisco announces the acquisition of Splunk for $28 billion to bolster its data analytics, AI observability, and cybersecurity portfolios.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-qcom" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">QCOM</span>
<span class="ticker-title">QUALCOMM Incorporated</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-qcom" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'qcom')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-qcom" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'qcom')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-qcom" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'qcom')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-qcom" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'qcom')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-qcom" onclick="toggleFullscreen('qcom')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-qcom
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_qcom = pd.read_csv("../downloads/qcom_enriched.csv")
df_qcom['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_qcom['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_qcom = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_qcom = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_qcom.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_qcom.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_qcom['date'], open=df_qcom['open'], high=df_qcom['high'], low=df_qcom['low'], close=df_qcom['close'],
        name='QCOM Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_qcom.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_qcom['date'], y=df_qcom['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_qcom, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_qcom = [{'date': '2019-04-16', 'label': 'Apple settlement rally', 'desc': 'Qualcomm and Apple reach an agreement to drop all worldwide litigation, signing a six-year patent license and chipset supply deal, triggering a 23% stock spike in one day.'}, {'date': '2021-06-30', 'label': 'Amon assumes CEO role', 'desc': "Cristiano Amon takes office as CEO, pushing Qualcomm's diversification into automotive chips, internet of things (IoT), and laptops."}, {'date': '2023-10-24', 'label': 'Snapdragon X Elite Launch', 'desc': "Qualcomm introduces Snapdragon X Elite, a custom ARM PC processor designed to rival Apple's M-series chips, driving optimism in the PC market."}]

for ev in events_qcom:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_qcom['date'][df_qcom['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_qcom.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_qcom[df_qcom['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_qcom['high'].mean()
        
        fig_qcom.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_qcom.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_qcom.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_qcom.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view QUALCOMM Incorporated Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>QUALCOMM Incorporated was founded in 1985 by Irwin Jacobs and a group of industry pioneers. Qualcomm pioneered CDMA wireless technology, which became the foundation for 3G, 4G, and 5G cellular communication networks. It is a leading designer of mobile system-on-chips (SoC) and cellular baseband processors.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2019-04-16 (Apple settlement rally):</strong> Qualcomm and Apple reach an agreement to drop all worldwide litigation, signing a six-year patent license and chipset supply deal, triggering a 23% stock spike in one day.</li>
      <li><strong>2021-06-30 (Amon assumes CEO role):</strong> Cristiano Amon takes office as CEO, pushing Qualcomm's diversification into automotive chips, internet of things (IoT), and laptops.</li>
      <li><strong>2023-10-24 (Snapdragon X Elite Launch):</strong> Qualcomm introduces Snapdragon X Elite, a custom ARM PC processor designed to rival Apple's M-series chips, driving optimism in the PC market.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Meta Platforms, Inc. (META)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-meta" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">META</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Meta Platforms, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-meta" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'meta')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-meta" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'meta')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-meta" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'meta')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-meta" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'meta')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-meta" onclick="toggleFullscreen('meta')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-meta
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_meta = pd.read_csv("../downloads/meta_enriched.csv")
df_meta['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_meta['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_meta = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_meta = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_meta.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_meta.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_meta['date'], open=df_meta['open'], high=df_meta['high'], low=df_meta['low'], close=df_meta['close'],
        name='META Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_meta.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_meta['date'], y=df_meta['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_meta, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_meta = [{'date': '2012-05-18', 'label': 'Facebook IPO', 'desc': 'Facebook goes public at $38 per share, in one of the largest tech IPOs in history, valuation initially peaking around $104 billion.'}, {'date': '2014-02-19', 'label': 'WhatsApp Acquisition', 'desc': 'Facebook announces the acquisition of mobile messaging app WhatsApp for $19 billion, cementing its mobile messaging leadership.'}, {'date': '2021-10-28', 'label': 'Rebranding to Meta', 'desc': 'Mark Zuckerberg announces the corporate name change to Meta Platforms to signal a focus on building virtual environments.'}, {'date': '2022-11-09', 'label': 'Layoffs & Efficiency Pivot', 'desc': 'Meta announces its first mass layoff of 11,000 employees, initiating a transition to capital discipline and cost reductions, fueling a massive stock recovery.'}]

for ev in events_meta:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_meta['date'][df_meta['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_meta.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_meta[df_meta['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_meta['high'].mean()
        
        fig_meta.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_meta.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_meta.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_meta.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Meta Platforms, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Meta Platforms, Inc. (formerly Facebook) was founded in 2004 by Mark Zuckerberg and his Harvard roommates. Starting as a social network, it acquired Instagram, WhatsApp, and Oculus. In 2021, the company rebranded to Meta to emphasize its focus on the metaverse and next-generation spatial computing.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2012-05-18 (Facebook IPO):</strong> Facebook goes public at $38 per share, in one of the largest tech IPOs in history, valuation initially peaking around $104 billion.</li>
      <li><strong>2014-02-19 (WhatsApp Acquisition):</strong> Facebook announces the acquisition of mobile messaging app WhatsApp for $19 billion, cementing its mobile messaging leadership.</li>
      <li><strong>2021-10-28 (Rebranding to Meta):</strong> Mark Zuckerberg announces the corporate name change to Meta Platforms to signal a focus on building virtual environments.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-11-09 (Layoffs & Efficiency Pivot):</strong> Meta announces its first mass layoff of 11,000 employees, initiating a transition to capital discipline and cost reductions, fueling a massive stock recovery.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-amzn" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">AMZN</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Amazon.com, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-amzn" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'amzn')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-amzn" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'amzn')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-amzn" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'amzn')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-amzn" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'amzn')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-amzn" onclick="toggleFullscreen('amzn')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-amzn
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_amzn = pd.read_csv("../downloads/amzn_enriched.csv")
df_amzn['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_amzn['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_amzn = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_amzn = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_amzn.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_amzn.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_amzn['date'], open=df_amzn['open'], high=df_amzn['high'], low=df_amzn['low'], close=df_amzn['close'],
        name='AMZN Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_amzn.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_amzn['date'], y=df_amzn['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_amzn, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_amzn = [{'date': '2017-06-16', 'label': 'Whole Foods Acquisition', 'desc': 'Amazon acquires Whole Foods Market for $13.7 billion, signaling a major expansion into brick-and-mortar grocery retail.'}, {'date': '2021-07-05', 'label': 'Bezos steps down / Jassy CEO', 'desc': 'Jeff Bezos officially steps down as CEO to become Executive Chairman; AWS chief Andy Jassy is appointed CEO.'}, {'date': '2022-06-06', 'label': '20-for-1 Stock Split', 'desc': 'Amazon implements a 20-for-1 stock split and initiates a $10 billion share buyback program.'}]

for ev in events_amzn:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_amzn['date'][df_amzn['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_amzn.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_amzn[df_amzn['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_amzn['high'].mean()
        
        fig_amzn.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_amzn.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_amzn.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_amzn.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Amazon.com, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Amazon.com, Inc. was founded in 1994 by Jeff Bezos as an online bookstore. It grew into the world's largest e-commerce platform and a dominant provider of cloud computing services via Amazon Web Services (AWS). Amazon also operates major digital streaming and smart home technology businesses.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2017-06-16 (Whole Foods Acquisition):</strong> Amazon acquires Whole Foods Market for $13.7 billion, signaling a major expansion into brick-and-mortar grocery retail.</li>
      <li><strong>2021-07-05 (Bezos steps down / Jassy CEO):</strong> Jeff Bezos officially steps down as CEO to become Executive Chairman; AWS chief Andy Jassy is appointed CEO.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-06-06 (20-for-1 Stock Split):</strong> Amazon implements a 20-for-1 stock split and initiates a $10 billion share buyback program.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

## Tesla, Inc. (TSLA)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-tsla" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">TSLA</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Tesla, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-tsla" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'tsla')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-tsla" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'tsla')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-tsla" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'tsla')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-tsla" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'tsla')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-tsla" onclick="toggleFullscreen('tsla')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-tsla
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_tsla = pd.read_csv("../downloads/tsla_enriched.csv")
df_tsla['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_tsla['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_tsla = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_tsla = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_tsla.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_tsla.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_tsla['date'], open=df_tsla['open'], high=df_tsla['high'], low=df_tsla['low'], close=df_tsla['close'],
        name='TSLA Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_tsla.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_tsla['date'], y=df_tsla['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_tsla, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_tsla = [{'date': '2012-06-22', 'label': 'Model S Deliveries Begin', 'desc': "Tesla begins deliveries of its Model S sedan, the vehicle that established Tesla's premium brand and manufacturing capabilities."}, {'date': '2020-08-31', 'label': '5-for-1 Stock Split', 'desc': 'Tesla splits its stock 5-for-1 to handle the rapid run-up in its share price.'}, {'date': '2020-12-21', 'label': 'S&P 500 Inclusion', 'desc': 'Tesla joins the S&P 500 index after consecutive quarters of profitability, triggering heavy institutional index-buying.'}, {'date': '2022-10-27', 'label': 'Twitter Acquisition closes', 'desc': 'Elon Musk completes the purchase of Twitter, leading to selloffs of TSLA shares to fund the acquisition and concerns over executive focus.'}]

for ev in events_tsla:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_tsla['date'][df_tsla['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_tsla.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_tsla[df_tsla['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_tsla['high'].mean()
        
        fig_tsla.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_tsla.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_tsla.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_tsla.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Tesla, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Tesla, Inc. was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning, with Elon Musk joining as lead investor and CEO shortly after. Tesla revolutionized the automotive market by proving electric vehicles could be fast, desirable, and practical, expanding into solar energy and battery storage systems.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2012-06-22 (Model S Deliveries Begin):</strong> Tesla begins deliveries of its Model S sedan, the vehicle that established Tesla's premium brand and manufacturing capabilities.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-08-31 (5-for-1 Stock Split):</strong> Tesla splits its stock 5-for-1 to handle the rapid run-up in its share price.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-12-21 (S&P 500 Inclusion):</strong> Tesla joins the S&P 500 index after consecutive quarters of profitability, triggering heavy institutional index-buying.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-10-27 (Twitter Acquisition closes):</strong> Elon Musk completes the purchase of Twitter, leading to selloffs of TSLA shares to fund the acquisition and concerns over executive focus.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-amd" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">AMD</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-amd" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'amd')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-amd" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'amd')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-amd" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'amd')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-amd" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'amd')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-amd" onclick="toggleFullscreen('amd')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-amd
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_amd = pd.read_csv("../downloads/amd_enriched.csv")
df_amd['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_amd['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_amd = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_amd = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_amd.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_amd.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_amd['date'], open=df_amd['open'], high=df_amd['high'], low=df_amd['low'], close=df_amd['close'],
        name='AMD Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_amd.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_amd['date'], y=df_amd['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_amd, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_amd = [{'date': '2014-10-08', 'label': 'Dr. Lisa Su becomes CEO', 'desc': 'Dr. Lisa Su is appointed President and CEO, guiding AMD from near-bankruptcy to technological leadership and financial stability.'}, {'date': '2017-03-02', 'label': 'Zen architecture launch', 'desc': "AMD launches its new Ryzen desktop processors based on the Zen architecture, matching Intel's performance and triggering a multi-year market share recovery."}, {'date': '2022-02-14', 'label': 'Xilinx Acquisition closed', 'desc': 'AMD completes the acquisition of adaptive computing leader Xilinx for a record $49 billion, expanding its data center presence.'}]

for ev in events_amd:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_amd['date'][df_amd['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_amd.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_amd[df_amd['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_amd['high'].mean()
        
        fig_amd.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_amd.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_amd.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_amd.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. was founded in 1969 by Jerry Sanders and colleagues. Initially a second-source manufacturer of microchips, AMD became Intel's primary competitor in x86 microprocessors and a major graphics processing unit (GPU) supplier through its acquisition of ATI Technologies in 2006.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2014-10-08 (Dr. Lisa Su becomes CEO):</strong> Dr. Lisa Su is appointed President and CEO, guiding AMD from near-bankruptcy to technological leadership and financial stability.</li>
      <li><strong>2017-03-02 (Zen architecture launch):</strong> AMD launches its new Ryzen desktop processors based on the Zen architecture, matching Intel's performance and triggering a multi-year market share recovery.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-02-14 (Xilinx Acquisition closed):</strong> AMD completes the acquisition of adaptive computing leader Xilinx for a record $49 billion, expanding its data center presence.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>


## Netflix, Inc. (NFLX)

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container-nflx" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">NFLX</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Netflix, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma-nflx" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma', 'nflx')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb-nflx" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb', 'nflx')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all-nflx" onclick="toggleIndicators('all', 'nflx')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear-nflx" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear', 'nflx')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn-nflx" onclick="toggleFullscreen('nflx')">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: chart-nflx
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load dataset
df_nflx = pd.read_csv("../downloads/nflx_enriched.csv")
df_nflx['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_nflx['date'])

# Create subplots
fig_nflx = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]
)

colors_nflx = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df_nflx.iterrows()]

# 1. Candlestick
fig_nflx.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df_nflx['date'], open=df_nflx['open'], high=df_nflx['high'], low=df_nflx['low'], close=df_nflx['close'],
        name='NFLX Price', increasing_line_color='#26a69a', decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a', decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350', showlegend=True
    ), row=1, col=1
)

# 2. SMAs
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['sma_20'], line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5), name='SMA 20', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['sma_50'], line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5), name='SMA 50', visible=True), row=1, col=1)
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['sma_200'], line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5), name='SMA 200', visible=True), row=1, col=1)

# 3. Bollinger Bands
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['bb_upper'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), name='BB Upper', visible=False), row=1, col=1)
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['bb_lower'], line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'), fill='tonexty', fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)', name='BB Lower', visible=False), row=1, col=1)

# 4. Volume
fig_nflx.add_trace(go.Bar(x=df_nflx['date'], y=df_nflx['volume'], name='Volume', marker=dict(color=colors_nflx, line=dict(width=0)), opacity=1.0, showlegend=False), row=2, col=1)

# 5. Overlays (Historical Events)
events_nflx = [{'date': '2013-02-01', 'label': 'House of Cards launch', 'desc': "Netflix releases its first major original drama series, 'House of Cards', proving the viability of streaming-only original programming."}, {'date': '2020-03-16', 'label': 'COVID Lockdown surge', 'desc': 'Pandemic lock-downs drive a historic wave of new signups and viewing hours, pushing Netflix to peak subscriber growth.'}, {'date': '2022-04-19', 'label': 'First subscriber loss', 'desc': 'Netflix reports a loss of 200,000 subscribers—its first quarterly decline in a decade—prompting a pivot to ad-supported plans and password-sharing crackdowns.'}]

for ev in events_nflx:
    ev_date = pd.to_datetime(ev['date'])
    # Get closest trading date to prevent empty gaps on weekend/holiday events
    trading_dates = df_nflx['date'][df_nflx['date'] >= ev_date]
    if not trading_dates.empty:
        actual_date = trading_dates.iloc[0]
        # Draw vertical line
        fig_nflx.add_vline(x=actual_date, line_width=1.2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888888", row=1, col=1)
        
        # Position label above the high price for that date
        row_data = df_nflx[df_nflx['date'] == actual_date]
        y_val = row_data['high'].values[0] if not row_data.empty else df_nflx['high'].mean()
        
        fig_nflx.add_annotation(
            x=actual_date,
            y=y_val,
            text=ev['label'],
            showarrow=True,
            arrowhead=1,
            arrowsize=1,
            arrowwidth=1,
            arrowcolor='#888888',
            ax=0,
            ay=-40,
            font=dict(color='#ffffff', size=9),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1,
            borderpad=4,
            row=1, col=1
        )

fig_nflx.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark', paper_bgcolor='#0c101b', plot_bgcolor='#0c101b', autosize=True, dragmode='pan', margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824', activecolor='#2962ff', font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11), bordercolor='#202637', borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', tickprefix='$', title='Price (USD)', fixedrange=True),
    yaxis2=dict(gridcolor='#202637', zerolinecolor='#202637', title='Volume', fixedrange=True),
    legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02, xanchor='right', x=1, bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
)
fig_nflx.update_xaxes(minallowed='2010-06-15', maxallowed='2026-07-05')
fig_nflx.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<details class="event-details">
  <summary>🔍 Click to view Netflix, Inc. Origins & Major Price Action Drivers</summary>
  <div class="event-content">
    <h4>Company Origins</h4>
    <p>Netflix, Inc. was founded in 1997 by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph as a DVD-by-mail service. Netflix launched streaming media in 2007, pioneering subscription video-on-demand. It has expanded into one of the largest media production companies in the world, distributing original content globally.</p>
    
    <h4>Key Historical Milestones & Price Events</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>2013-02-01 (House of Cards launch):</strong> Netflix releases its first major original drama series, 'House of Cards', proving the viability of streaming-only original programming.</li>
      <li><strong>2020-03-16 (COVID Lockdown surge):</strong> Pandemic lock-downs drive a historic wave of new signups and viewing hours, pushing Netflix to peak subscriber growth.</li>
      <li><strong>2022-04-19 (First subscriber loss):</strong> Netflix reports a loss of 200,000 subscribers—its first quarterly decline in a decade—prompting a pivot to ad-supported plans and password-sharing crackdowns.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</details>
:::

<hr/>

<script>
function toggleFullscreen(symbol) {
  var el = document.getElementById('chart-container-' + symbol);
  el.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
  var btn = document.getElementById('fullscreen-btn-' + symbol);
  if (el.classList.contains('fullscreen')) {
    btn.innerHTML = "🗖 Exit Full Screen";
  } else {
    btn.innerHTML = "⛶ Full Screen";
  }
  // Let Plotly adjust to new size (after transition)
  setTimeout(function() {
    window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'));
  }, 150);
}

function toggleIndicators(type, symbol) {
  var container = document.getElementById('chart-container-' + symbol);
  var gd = container.querySelector('.plotly-graph-div');
  if (!gd) return;
  
  var buttons = container.querySelectorAll('.indicator-buttons .tool-btn');
  buttons.forEach(function(btn) {
    btn.classList.remove('active');
  });
  
  var activeBtn = document.getElementById('btn-' + type + '-' + symbol);
  if (activeBtn) {
    activeBtn.classList.add('active');
  }
  
  if (type === 'sma') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [1, 2, 3]);
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'bb') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [1, 2, 3]);
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'all') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'clear') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
  }
}
</script>

</body>
